questions.questionText: The rate-limiting enzyme of cholesterol synthesis is:
questions.explanation: HMG-CoA reductase is the rate-limiting enzyme of cholesterol synthesis and the pharmacologic target of statins. HMG-CoA synthase is involved earlier and mitochondrial isoform is important in ketogenesis. Acetyl-CoA carboxylase is rate limiting for fatty acid synthesis. Hormone-sensitive lipase mobilizes stored triglyceride.

questions.questionText: Carnitine deficiency impairs transport of which molecule into mitochondria?
questions.explanation: Carnitine is required for transport of long-chain fatty acyl groups into mitochondria for beta-oxidation. Malonyl-CoA actually inhibits carnitine palmitoyltransferase I. Ketone bodies do not require carnitine transport. Acetyl-CoA from glycolysis enters indirectly via pyruvate metabolism, not by the carnitine shuttle.
questions.source: Lippincott Illustrated Reviews: Biochemistry, Lipid Metabolism

questions.questionText: Abetalipoproteinemia is characterized by deficiency of:
questions.explanation: Abetalipoproteinemia results from inability to form ApoB-containing lipoproteins, leading to fat malabsorption, acanthocytosis, and vitamin E deficiency. ApoA-I is the major HDL apoprotein. LCAT deficiency causes corneal clouding and hemolysis. Lipoprotein lipase deficiency causes hyperchylomicronemia.
